name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro (Think High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (1.6T total / 49B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention, Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections, Muon optimizer
note: Fast/low-latency mode of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro preview checkpoint, the default reasoning tier a user gets without selecting anything. (middle reasoning-effort tier, called 'Think High' on the official HF model-card benchmark table).

results.notes: Think-High mode, per macaron.im's transcription of DeepSeek's technical report
Terminal-Bench 2.0, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
BrowseComp Pass@1, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 12.4% / Score 27.6% (rank 40, listed as 'deepseek-v4-pro'). Mapped to the 'Think High' catalog variant (vs. 'Non-Think' or 'Think Max') as the closest match to the leaderboard's 'High' effort label -- flagged as a moderate-confidence inference since the leaderboard doesn't use DeepSeek's own 'Think High' naming verbatim.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via DeepSeek's own official API-docs news post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news260424/, dated 2026-04-24) announcing the V4-Pro/V4-Flash preview, and via the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro Hugging Face model card, which gives 1.6T total/49B active params, MIT license, 1M-token context, and a benchmark table covering three reasoning tiers (Non-Think/Think High/Think Max). Numerous SEO/content-farm sites (codersera, sitepoint, morphllm, etc.) published speculative-sounding 'release timeline' articles with suspiciously precise but unverifiable claims (e.g. percentile rankings, an 'Intelligence Index' score) — those were NOT used; all numbers here come from the official HF model card table.

2026-07-16: Checked all five flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) against (1) DeepSeek's own official sources -- the HF model card and the V4 tech report (arXiv:2606.19348), whose Non-Think/Think-High/Think-Max comparison table reports MMLU-Pro (not base MMLU), HMMT-2026-Feb and IMOAnswerBench (not MATH or AIME), and omits HumanEval and MMMU entirely -- (2) independent trackers Vals.ai (model page lists only "MMLU Pro", no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/MMMU), Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Breakdown for this model line has no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval entries; only MMMU-Pro, a distinct slug, appears), and NIST's independent CAISI evaluation (uses OTIS-AIME-2025/PUMaC/SMT harder competition-math sets against the Think-Max tier, not plain AIME/MATH, and doesn't test Think-High at all), and (3) sibling family files (V4-Pro Think-Max, V4-Pro Non-Think, V4-Flash Think-High), none of which carry these five slugs either -- the same substitution pattern across every source. A third-party transcription (macaron.im) does list an MMLU=90.1 and HumanEval=76.8 figure, but explicitly labeled for the pretrained "V4-Pro-Base" checkpoint, a different model entity from the instruction-tuned/RL'd reasoning-tier chat variant catalogued here, so it was not used. mmmu is additionally inapplicable on capability grounds: this checkpoint is text-only (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false per the HF model card), so a vision benchmark cannot apply. All five are treated as legitimate retired/inapplicable-benchmark absences (DeepSeek-V4 has moved to MMLU-Pro and HMMT/IMOAnswerBench as its knowledge/math evals), not data gaps -- no scores added.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep pass, mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Chased all four gaps through the six new source types specified for this pass, none of the first pass's checklist (official launch page/system card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com) repeated.

1. Stanford HELM -- loaded the actual leaderboard tables directly in-browser (not just search snippets) for HELM Classic MMLU (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest), HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest, which does carry plain 'MMLU - EM' and 'MATH - Equivalent (CoT)' columns), and HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest, which uses MMLU-Pro/Omni-MATH instead of MMLU/MATH). All three leaderboards are stale relative to this model's 2026-04-24 release: HELM Classic MMLU's top/most-recent entry is Claude 3.5 Sonnet (20241022) with only 'DeepSeek v3' present; HELM Lite's top entry is GPT-4o (2024-05-13), again only 'DeepSeek v3'; HELM Capabilities' most recent entries top out around GPT-5.1/GPT-5 (Aug-Nov 2025), with only 'DeepSeek-R1-0528' and 'DeepSeek v3' as DeepSeek entries. No DeepSeek-V4-Pro (or any V4 variant) row exists on any HELM leaderboard -- HELM has not evaluated this model. Confirmed absence, not merely unsearched.

2. arXiv / Semantic Scholar baselines -- multiple targeted searches ('DeepSeek-V4-Pro MMLU site:arxiv.org', 'DeepSeek-V4 benchmark comparison AIME HumanEval arxiv 2026', semantic-scholar MMLU baseline searches) surfaced only the model's own technical report (arXiv:2606.19348, already the primary source in this file) and a long tail of SEO/content-farm blogs (macaron.im, notelm.ai, redreamality.com, bswen.com, tokenmix.ai, etc.) making vague, uncited claims like 'MMLU ~89%' or 'HumanEval ~96.4%' with no paper, table, or reproducible source behind them -- textbook fabricated-precision content-farm noise, consistent with what the first pass already flagged and excluded. No genuine third-party arXiv/Semantic Scholar paper using this exact model as a cited baseline with an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number was found.

3. GitHub eval-harness logs -- checked EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness and the community 'HenryZ838978/deepseek-harness' repo (270+ trials, 16 protocol quirks) directly; the latter is a protocol/API-behavior characterization tool (streaming chunk counts, cache-hit ratios), not a benchmark-scoring harness, and contains no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers. No lm-eval-harness results.json or comparable committed run log for this model was found.

4. Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's deepseek-v4-pro page lists reasoning-effort tiers but no benchmark scores at all; llm-stats.com's live top-15/331 leaderboard view does not surface a DeepSeek V4 Pro row; NVIDIA NIM catalog page is cookie-gated with no benchmark content visible; Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard explicitly has no DeepSeek V4 entry (confirmed via search: 'Scale's SEAL leaderboard has no DeepSeek V4 result'); PapersWithCode's MMLU SOTA page redirected away and surfaced no DeepSeek-V4-Pro entry. LiveBench.ai searches returned no direct DeepSeek-V4-Pro results page, only indirect third-party mentions.

5. Non-English sources -- searched for Chinese/Korean/Japanese independent testing/blog coverage; nothing beyond the same English-language content-farm results resurfacing; no region-specific independent MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers found for this model.

6. Sibling/family comparison tables -- re-examined the HF model card's own 'DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max vs Frontier Models' table (Opus-4.6, GPT-5.4, Gemini-3.1-Pro, K2.6, GLM-5.1, DS-V4-Pro-Max), confirming via direct page-text extraction that its columns are MMLU-Pro, SimpleQA-Verified, Chinese-SimpleQA, GPQA Diamond, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces, HMMT -- no MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval column exists in that comparison table (also confirms this is the Think-Max tier, not Think-High, and is the same HF page already used, not an independent sibling source). No other sibling-model launch page/tech-report comparison table containing this exact model's MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval score was located.

Re-confirmed via direct page text (not just search-snippet summaries) that the HF model card's 'Base Model' evaluation table -- the only place on the card carrying plain MMLU (90.1), MATH (64.5, 4-shot EM), and HumanEval (76.8, Pass@1) figures -- is explicitly for 'DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Base', the pretrained (non-instruct, non-reasoning-tier) checkpoint, structurally separate from the 'Instruct Model' section's Non-Think/Think High/Think Max tiers. This reconfirms the first pass's finding rather than providing new grounds to use it for the Think-High variant. No AIME entry exists anywhere on the card for either the Base or Instruct tables (the instruct tiers use HMMT-2026-Feb and IMOAnswerBench in place of AIME/MATH, as already documented).

Conclusion: all four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain confirmed absent after checking all 6 new source types (exceeding the required minimum of 3). No credible, citable, independent score exists for this exact model variant on any of these four benchmarks. Treated as legitimate omitted_no_evidence outcomes -- no scores added, no changes to the results array.

2026-07-18 (round-22, mmmu gap): Re-checked the single flagged gap, mmmu, per this pass's instruction to first verify modality before searching further. Corpus fields confirm this checkpoint is text-only: "modalities": ["text"] and "capabilities": {"vision": false}. Re-inspected the HF model card's full "Instruct Model" benchmark table (Non-Think/Think High/Think Max tiers) directly -- it contains no image-input capability, no vision modality note, and no MMMU (or MMMU-Pro) column anywhere on the card, consistent with a text-only reasoning-tier chat model. MMMU is a vision-reasoning benchmark requiring image inputs, which this model architecturally cannot accept -- this is not an unpublished-but-existing score, it is a benchmark that cannot be administered to this model at all. This matches and reconfirms the identical conclusion already recorded in the 2026-07-16 verificationNotes entry ("mmmu is additionally inapplicable on capability grounds"). No score added; no further independent-tracker/arXiv/sibling-table search was necessary given the unambiguous capability-field confirmation, per the task's own instruction to skip further searching once text-only status is confirmed.
